Name: ZK Infrastructure Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What happens if d/acc-aligned capital doesn't fund ZK development?
If We Don't Invest: ZK development continues but driven by VC seeking returns; privacy features deprioritized for speed-to-market
What Fills The Vacuum: Enterprise ZK solutions (IBM, Microsoft); compliance-first ZK (Chainalysis-friendly); centralized prover networks
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Negative - ZK becomes surveillance-compatible infrastructure rather than privacy-preserving
Strategic Implication: Critical to fund - vacuum would be filled by captured alternatives

Name: Decentralized Identity Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if self-sovereign identity doesn't achieve adoption?
If We Don't Invest: Government digital ID systems and Big Tech identity providers become default
What Fills The Vacuum: Apple/Google passkeys; EU digital identity wallet (centralized); China-style social credit systems
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Strongly Negative - identity becomes control infrastructure rather than user-empowering
Strategic Implication: High priority - counterfactual is clearly worse; timing matters

Name: FHE Development Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if FHE remains underfunded and doesn't reach performance thresholds?
If We Don't Invest: Institutions either accept on-chain transparency or stay off-chain entirely
What Fills The Vacuum: Trusted execution environments (Intel SGX successors); permissioned chains; traditional finance rails
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Moderately Negative - privacy-preserving computation doesn't develop; institutions stay TradFi
Strategic Implication: Important but timing uncertain - monitor performance trajectory

Name: Decentralized Stablecoin Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if decentralized stablecoins fail to scale?
If We Don't Invest: Centralized stablecoins (USDC, USDT) dominate entirely; regulatory capture complete
What Fills The Vacuum: CBDC integration; fully surveilled payment rails; bank-issued stablecoins
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Strongly Negative - monetary infrastructure becomes surveillance tool
Strategic Implication: Moderate priority - market size justifies multiple attempts; failure mode is bad

Name: Oracle Decentralization Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if decentralized oracle alternatives don't compete with Chainlink?
If We Don't Invest: Chainlink achieves near-monopoly; single point of failure for DeFi pricing
What Fills The Vacuum: Chainlink-only ecosystem; exchange-native price feeds; centralized data providers
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Mildly Negative - concentration risk but Chainlink itself isn't captured (yet)
Strategic Implication: Lower priority - incumbent isn't actively bad; monitoring sufficient

Name: Quadratic Funding Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if QF/retroPGF mechanisms don't develop further?
If We Don't Invest: Public goods funding returns to grants from foundations and governments
What Fills The Vacuum: Centralized grant committees; corporate sponsorship; traditional philanthropy
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Mildly Negative - coordination primitive doesn't develop but alternatives aren't terrible
Strategic Implication: Speculative - valuable if works but counterfactual isn't catastrophic

Name: Data Availability Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if DA layers fail and rollups stay on Ethereum mainnet?
If We Don't Invest: L2 costs remain high; scaling limited; Ethereum more centralized (hardware requirements)
What Fills The Vacuum: Ethereum-only DA (expensive but works); highly centralized validiums; centralized rollups
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Mildly Negative - scaling slower but Ethereum DA isn't captured
Strategic Implication: Moderate priority - competition healthy; multiple funded players already

Name: Cross-Chain Bridge Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if secure bridges don't develop and chains remain isolated?
If We Don't Invest: Users stay on single chains; liquidity fragmented; CEXs become cross-chain layer
What Fills The Vacuum: Centralized exchange bridging (Coinbase as bridge); custodial solutions; walled gardens
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Moderately Negative - interoperability layer becomes centralized
Strategic Implication: Important but risky - bridge security track record is terrible

Name: Privacy L2 Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if Aztec and other privacy L2s fail?
If We Don't Invest: DeFi remains fully transparent; financial privacy only via centralized services
What Fills The Vacuum: Compliant privacy (Chainalysis-approved mixing); CeFi privacy; offshore banking
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Moderately Negative - on-chain privacy doesn't exist; TradFi remains privacy option
Strategic Implication: High priority - counterfactual clearly worse; strong d/acc alignment

Name: DAO Tooling Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if DAO governance tools don't improve?
If We Don't Invest: DAOs remain ineffective; protocols revert to foundation/team control
What Fills The Vacuum: Centralized protocol governance; benevolent dictator model; corporate structures
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Mildly Negative - decentralized governance fails but centralized alternatives work
Strategic Implication: Lower priority - current DAOs functional enough; improvement is incremental

Name: Sybil Resistance Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if Sybil resistance remains unsolved?
If We Don't Invest: Democratic mechanisms (QF, voting) remain gameable; plutocracy persists
What Fills The Vacuum: One-token-one-vote dominance; KYC-gated governance; platform-controlled identity
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Moderately Negative - democratic primitives don't work; identity centralizes
Strategic Implication: Important - prerequisite for other d/acc mechanisms

Name: Account Abstraction Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if smart wallets don't achieve adoption?
If We Don't Invest: Users remain on EOAs; key management stays hard; social recovery doesn't scale
What Fills The Vacuum: Centralized custody (Coinbase); custodial wallets; bank-integrated crypto
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Moderately Negative - self-custody remains expert-only; centralized custody wins
Strategic Implication: Moderate priority - UX improvement with d/acc benefits; competitive market

Name: MEV Protection Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if MEV protection doesn't improve?
If We Don't Invest: Users continue losing value to MEV extraction; sophisticated actors dominate
What Fills The Vacuum: Centralized order flow (payment for order flow model); private mempools; CeFi execution
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Moderately Negative - value extraction continues; retail disadvantaged
Strategic Implication: Moderate priority - user protection important; active development ongoing

Name: ZK Identity Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if ZK identity (Polygon ID, Semaphore) doesn't scale?
If We Don't Invest: Identity verification requires full disclosure; privacy/compliance tradeoff unresolved
What Fills The Vacuum: KYC everywhere; identity-linked transactions; financial surveillance
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Strongly Negative - privacy and compliance become mutually exclusive
Strategic Implication: High priority - unique capability; strong d/acc alignment; Buenos Aires shows path

Name: Decentralized Sequencer Vacuum
Counterfactual Question: What if L2 sequencers remain centralized?
If We Don't Invest: L2s remain censorship-capable; single points of failure persist; trust in teams required
What Fills The Vacuum: Centralized but 'trusted' sequencers; regulated sequencer operators; KYC at sequencer level
Net D/acc Impact of Inaction: Moderately Negative - L2s don't achieve L1 properties; trust assumptions remain
Strategic Implication: Important - fundamental to L2 value proposition; timeline critical
